stories.title: Nolan Wells' cause and manner of death undetermined, attorney says, citing family's autopsy
stories.summary: Nolan Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi teen, died under undetermined circumstances, according to a preliminary independent autopsy. The autopsy found red discoloration on the back of his head, but the cause remains unknown. Wells' body was found on July 6, six days after he went missing on a Fourth of July trip to Horn Island. The official autopsy results from the state medical examiner's office have not yet been released, pending further investigation.

stories.title: Arrowhead: Stock Up On SHTG Data - No Ceiling On Valuation Growth Potential (NASDAQ:ARWR)
stories.summary: Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals stock surged nearly 20% after strong Phase 3 results for its lipid-lowering agent, plozasiran, showing significant triglyceride reductions and risk reduction for acute pancreatitis. The company's quarterly dosing and favorable safety profile position it to challenge Ionis in the lucrative sHTG market. Regulatory filings are planned before year-end. The stock reached a value of $88.6 per share.

stories.title: Live updates: Trump attends dignified transfer of U.S. service members
stories.summary: Four US service members killed in an Iranian attack on a US air base in Jordan on July 17 were honored in a dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware. President Trump met with the families of the fallen service members, including Sgt. Angel Rampersad, Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales. The four were assigned to air defense units, risking their lives to protect others from incoming attacks. The transfer comes as tensions with Iran remain high.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: Marvel's Wonder Man, Simon Williams, faces embezzlement and murder charges, marking a significant turn in his career. The charges come as Wonder Man's career takes a nosedive, with preview pages showing him confronting his agent over the allegations. The consequences of these charges are unclear, but they signal a major shift in Wonder Man's public image. The next development in this story is expected to unfold in the comic book series, with the latest issue hitting stores this Wednesday.

stories.title: Sestao River - Athletic Club: horario y dónde ver hoy en TV el partido amistoso de pretemporada
stories.summary: The Athletic Club is set to face off against Sestao River in a pre-season friendly match at Las Llanas on Wednesday, July 22, at 19:30 hours. The match will be a test of the team's physical and tactical progression, following a 0-11 victory over SD Leioa in their previous friendly. The Sestao River presents a challenge with a higher level of competition, but the Athletic Club's coach, Edin Terzic, aims to continue accumulating kilometers of training and implementing new tactics. The match will be broadcast live on the Athletic Club's official streaming channel.

stories.title: Pittsburgh Pirates at New York Yankees Preview - 07/21/2026
stories.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates face the New York Yankees at Yankee Stadium on July 22, 2026, at 1:35 p.m. ET. Pirates' Mitch Keller returns to the mound after a nearly two-week absence, seeking to bounce back from a poor performance against the Atlanta Braves. Yankees' Garrett Cole pitches against the Pirates for the third time in his career, looking to end a two-start losing streak. Cole's last win came on July 3.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina react to final defeat and Messi questions
stories.summary: Argentina's World Cup campaign ended in defeat to Spain, 1-0 in extra time, on Sunday in New Jersey. Midfielder Enzo Fernandez was sent off late in normal time for two yellow cards. Fernandez has since praised the team's effort, saying they represented Argentina "in the best possible way." The team's performance will be scrutinized following reports of incidents prior to the match.

stories.title: Why Jalen Brunson wasn't chosen as the NBA2K27 cover athlete over Victor Wembanyama
stories.summary: Victor Wembanyama has been chosen as the main cover athlete for NBA2K27. The 19-year-old Spurs center was selected despite the Knicks' recent NBA championship win, led by Jalen Brunson who was awarded the NBA Finals MVP award. Brunson's team defeated the Spurs in the NBA Finals, winning more clutch games. The NBA2K27 video game is set to release with Wembanyama as its cover athlete.

stories.title: Wemby, Rose, Clark are NBA 2k27 Cover Athletes
stories.summary: NBA 2K27 will feature Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose as cover athletes, with Wembanyama on the Standard Edition. The game is set to release on September 4. Wembanyama, a 22-year-old dominant player, led the San Antonio Spurs to the 2026 NBA Finals. The trio will be available on various editions of the game.

stories.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
stories.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's "Ali G: Who Iz I?" is set to release in theaters on October 23. The film marks Cohen's feature directorial debut, in which he also stars as the character Ali G. Cohen originated the role in 1998 for Channel 4's "The 11 O'Clock Show" and reprised it in his own "Da Ali G Show" in the early 2000s. The movie will be released by Amazon MGM Studios.

stories.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
stories.summary: Natalie Fleet has stepped down as safeguarding minister, citing the role as "overwhelmingly triggering" and affecting her health. She was appointed to the position on May 12 this year. Fleet, a Labour MP who was groomed and raped as a teenager, described her decision as the "bravest thing I've ever done." Her successor will now take on the responsibility of supporting victims and addressing the worst of humanity.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely include 12GB of RAM, an upgrade from the original 8GB. The new processor currently powers the iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max. The updated Neo may also come in new colors. The next model is expected to be more expensive due to ongoing component shortages.

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8 in 100 balls, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and Lauren Bell, who picked up key wickets. The win marks a strong start for the Brave in the competition.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of personal willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods high in sugar and refined carbohydrates to reduce obesity in Britain. According to the report, these foods are the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee is urging supermarkets and manufacturers to reformulate their products to reduce sugar content.

stories.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
stories.summary: Anti-doping tests at the Tour de France are being conducted at night for the first time, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am. This unusual approach is being justified as an exceptional measure to prevent doping, particularly peptide and testosterone micro dosing. The tests have already affected the race, with Dane Jonas Vingegaard crashing out after being tested in the middle of the night. The sport's efforts to move away from a chequered doping past are being closely watched.

stories.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
stories.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will carry the flag and lead Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games, having won gold in Birmingham and silver on the Gold Coast in 2018. Eccles will now lead a procession of athletes into the venue.

stories.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
stories.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has unveiled a new look at the spiral galaxy Messier 94, also known as NGC 4736, on June 30, 2026. The galaxy features a bright inner ring where new stars are forming, fueled by gas driven in its unique oval-shaped structure. This image combines X-rays of different wavelengths with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. The new look at Messier 94 is now available to experience through sound.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro joins Ben Stiller in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody," marking Del Toro's first lead series comedy role. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and awaiting trial. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." Del Toro's character details remain under wraps.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns, and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and their lawyers requested extradition to Russia. The Angolan authorities will now monitor the influence of Africa Politology, a Russian political network linked to the defendants, ahead of the election.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off on his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing a lack of authority to do so. Mamdani had previously called for Netanyahu's arrest over an International Criminal Court arrest warrant for alleged war crimes. The US is not an ICC member state, rendering the warrant unenforceable domestically. Mamdani now urges US officials to act on the warrant, as Netanyahu continues to visit the country.

stories.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
stories.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally charged in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of 65-year-old Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter, a British-South African mother of four, was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend. The woman, who was originally taken into custody in 2025, is now under formal investigation. A judicial inquiry was opened on May 7, 2025, leading to the formal charge.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
stories.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 out of 183 tested towers have tested positive for live legionella bacteria. Health officials are continuing to test the remaining towers, with results expected in the coming week. The city's health department will continue to monitor the situation and take further action as necessary.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: Susumu Tonegawa Dies at 86; Nobel Laureate Showed How Antibodies Fight Infection
stories.summary: The first Japanese scientist to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, he went on to discover how memories are stored, advancing understanding of how the brain works.
